SEARCH
Package com.example.eventlottery

Class AccessibilityUtils

java.lang.Object
com.example.eventlottery.AccessibilityUtils
public final class AccessibilityUtils extends Object
Utilities for adapting the UI when system-level touch exploration (TalkBack, Switch Access, etc.) is active. All methods are safe to call at any time — they are no-ops when accessibility is not enabled.

Layout guidelines for accessibility: use android:textSize in sp (scalable pixels) for all user-visible text so font scale / system settings apply; reserve dp for non-text sizing (icons, margins, minimum touch targets). Give every ImageView, ImageButton, and meaningful graphic a non-empty android:contentDescription (or a string resource reference in XML) so TalkBack can announce it.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static void
    enlargeSmallTouchTargets(android.content.Context context, android.view.View root)
    Walks every view in the hierarchy rooted at the given root view and, for any view that is clickable or focusable and smaller than 48 dp in either dimension, raises its minimum size to 48 dp.
    static boolean
    isTouchExplorationEnabled(android.content.Context context)
    Returns true when the user has an accessibility service with touch exploration active.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • isTouchExplorationEnabled

      public static boolean isTouchExplorationEnabled(android.content.Context context)
      Returns true when the user has an accessibility service with touch exploration active.
      Parameters:
      context - any Context used to reach the system AccessibilityManager
      Returns:
      true if touch exploration (e.g. TalkBack) is currently enabled

    • enlargeSmallTouchTargets

      public static void enlargeSmallTouchTargets(android.content.Context context, android.view.View root)
      Walks every view in the hierarchy rooted at the given root view and, for any view that is clickable or focusable and smaller than 48 dp in either dimension, raises its minimum size to 48 dp. Call this after the layout has been inflated (e.g. from onResume) so that view dimensions are already known, but it also works before first measure because setMinimumWidth/Height only sets a floor.
      Parameters:
      context - any Context used to check whether touch exploration is active
      root - root view of the hierarchy to traverse
